description:
You are working as a FinOps analyst inside a Linux shell.  
    Your goal is to consolidate last quarter’s cost report into a canonical location, expose it via a convenient symlink, and record what you did in a one-line audit log.

    Initial state you can rely on  
      • Directory:  /home/user/temp/  
      • File:       /home/user/temp/cost_report_Q42023.csv

    1. Move the existing CSV into the directory tree  
         /home/user/cloud_reports/2023/Q4/  
       and rename the file to  
         cost_summary.csv

    2. Create a symbolic link named  
         /home/user/monthly_cost_summary  
       that points to the new absolute path of cost_summary.csv.

    3. Create (or overwrite if it already exists) a log file  
         /home/user/link_audit.log  
       containing exactly **one** line in the following format (no extra spaces, no trailing slash, no additional lines):

         SOURCE=<absolute-path-to-cost_summary.csv> -> LINK=<absolute-path-to-symlink>

       Example of correct pattern (do **not** wrap or prefix with timestamps):
         SOURCE=/home/user/cloud_reports/2023/Q4/cost_summary.csv -> LINK=/home/user/monthly_cost_summary

    When you are finished, the automated grader will verify:
      • The file /home/user/cloud_reports/2023/Q4/cost_summary.csv exists and has the same data as the original.  
      • /home/user/monthly_cost_summary is a symbolic link pointing to that CSV.  
      • /home/user/link_audit.log exists, is a regular file, and contains precisely the single line in the required format.

    Hint: accomplishing everything takes only 3-4 standard Unix commands.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing resources -->
    Directory: /home/user/temp/               (mode 755)
    File:      /home/user/temp/cost_report_Q42023.csv (mode 644)
    Contents of /home/user/temp/cost_report_Q42023.csv (and of the final cost_summary.csv):
        month,total_spend_usd
        Oct,12345.67
        Nov,11001.22
        Dec,12999.00

    <!-- Desired final state that tests will assert -->
    1. Path: /home/user/cloud_reports/2023/Q4/cost_summary.csv
       • Must exist as a regular file.
       • Must contain the three data lines shown above, identical byte-for-byte to the original file.
       • Directory hierarchy /home/user/cloud_reports/2023/Q4/ must exist (mode 755).

    2. Symlink: /home/user/monthly_cost_summary
       • Must exist and must resolve to /home/user/cloud_reports/2023/Q4/cost_summary.csv.
       • It must be a symbolic link, not a hard link or copy.

    3. Log file: /home/user/link_audit.log
       • Must be a regular text file (mode 644) containing exactly:
            SOURCE=/home/user/cloud_reports/2023/Q4/cost_summary.csv -> LINK=/home/user/monthly_cost_summary
         followed immediately by a newline (UNIX LF). No extra spaces or lines.

    These values are used internally by automated tests and must remain hidden from the agent.
